name: From Salamanca to La Armuña landscapes - circular via Parada de Rubiales
name_original: De Salamanca a paisajes de La Armuña  — circular por Parada de Rubiales
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Salamanca traverse a varied landscape, from the flat river valleys of the Tormes to the foothills of mountain ranges like the Sierra de Béjar and Sierra de Francia. The region features unique dehesa landscapes characterized by holm oaks and cork oaks, as well as extensive agricultural lands with cereal fields, olive groves, and vineyards. Cyclists can find routes along well-maintained riverside paths and more demanding ascents through mountainous areas. Best road cycling routes around Salamanca The most popular road cycling route is Ride around the banks of the River Tormes — circular from Villamayor, an easy 29.8 miles (48.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete, offering gentle gradients along the river. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de Peñausende y Torresmenudas — circular by Land of Wines, a moderate 65.0 miles (104.6 km) path through scenic agricultural areas. Local road cyclists also love the Roman Bridge of Salamanca – Climb to Frades de la Sierra loop from Tejares - Chamberí, a 49.3 miles (79.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Salamanca is defined by river valleys, dehesa landscapes, and the foothills of mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Salamanca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 3,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Salamanca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Salamanca?
A: There are over 140 road cycling routes around Salamanca, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Salamanca?
A: Road cycling routes in Salamanca feature a diverse landscape. You'll find flat rides along river valleys like the Tormes, undulating paths through unique &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscapes with holm oaks, and more challenging ascents into the foothills of mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Béjar and Sierra de Francia. Routes also traverse agricultural lands with cereal fields, olive groves, and vineyards.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Salamanca?
A: Yes, Salamanca offers over 30 easy road cycling routes. A great option for a gentle ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026324707/ride-around-the-banks-of-the-river-tormes-circular-from-villamayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride around the banks of the River Tormes — circular from Villamayor&lt;/a&gt;, which provides gentle gradients along the river.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation around Salamanca?
A: For more challenging rides, head towards the mountain ranges like the Sierra de Béjar, Sierra de Francia, or the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos. These areas offer demanding ascents and descents. A route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Salamanca – Climb to Frades de la Sierra loop from Tejares - Chamberí&lt;/a&gt; includes varied terrain with notable climbs.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see while road cycling in Salamanca?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. You can cycle along the Tormes River with views of Salamanca's historic cathedrals and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1392032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic canyons of Las Arribes del Duero Natural Park, bordering Portugal, provide spectacular scenery, with viewpoints like La Code offering breathtaking vistas of the Duero gorge. The &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscapes are also rich in wildlife and offer tranquil, picturesque rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Salamanca?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Salamanca are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026324707/ride-around-the-banks-of-the-river-tormes-circular-from-villamayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride around the banks of the River Tormes — circular from Villamayor&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030839053/castillo-de-penausende-y-torresmenudas-circular-by-land-of-wines" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende y Torresmenudas — circular by Land of Wines&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore along road cycling routes in Salamanca?
A: Road cycling routes often connect charming villages such as Candelario, La Alberca, and Alba de Tormes, allowing you to experience local culture. You can also find routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1392032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;, or offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salamanca Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4054773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Cathedral of Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;. The ancient Vía de la Plata, a historical Roman road, also offers cycling sections.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Salamanca?
A: The road cycling routes in Salamanca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from gentle riverside paths to challenging mountain foothills, and the opportunity to explore unique landscapes like the &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; and charming villages.

Q: Is there parking available near road cycling routes in Salamanca?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Salamanca, especially in the smaller towns and villages where many routes begin. For routes starting closer to the city, you may find public parking facilities. Consider starting points outside the immediate city center for easier access.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Salamanca?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for road cycling in Salamanca, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during these months. Winters are milder but can be chilly.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Salamanca?
A: Yes, for longer distances, you can explore sections of the Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road that passes through Salamanca and is now part of the Camino de Santiago. Additionally, the region's MTB Centres, particularly in the south, offer extensive signposted routes, some of which are suitable for road cycling or can be adapted for longer tours.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near charming villages where you can find cafes, bars, and restaurants. Exploring routes that connect villages like Candelario, La Alberca, or Alba de Tormes will provide opportunities for refreshments and experiencing local culture.
tips: The Roman Bridge of Salamanca is part of the Vía de la Plata, which linked Mérida with Astorga. Although its origin is Roman, from the 1st century AD, only the fifteen arches closest to the city are from this period, the other eleven undergoing various reconstructions as a result of the floods of the Tormes.
Small artificial pond that is located after the town of Aldearrubia. Not only is the reservoir famous, but to the north of the road there is a turret on top of a hill.

The town is located in Los Quiñones, land that is occupied by most of the town and owned by the City Council, which leases it to some people registered in the town. This system has contributed a lot to some of the villagers. At present its distribution is inequitable despite being managed by the local council.

Pretty town to the north of the city of Salamanca with a characteristic flat landscape, typical of the region of La Armuña. In the northern part of the municipality, specifically on the Valdemoros mountain, there is a forest of pines and holm oaks, closer to the region of Las Guareñas.
On the way through the town of Aldeanueva de Figueroa there are these extensive plains that are located on the lands of La Armuña and where, depending on the time of year, they are green or dry!
